observations.recommendation: 3) While these unserviceable properties are not yet disposed, have the Accountant record such under Other Assets account.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that Local Chief
Executive:
a) Cause the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count on all
the PPE owned by the Municipality;
b) Instruct the same to identify the unserviceable properties and
record the same in the IIRUP to facilitate the disposal and eventual
dropping from the books; and
c) While these unserviceable properties are not yet disposed, have the
Accountant record such under Other Assets account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate that
activities be efficiently planned, that direct payment be resorted to
instead of payment thru reimbursement, and that the concerned office
be familiarized with the provisions of RA No. 9184 by attending
trainings on Procurement Law and effectively follow the provisions of
RA No. 9184 in procuring needs for the various activities.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Management:
a) Comply with the submission of Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption, with complete and proper documentary
requirements, within ten (10) days of the succeeding month
pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 and Section 361 of GAAM;
b) Submit a Monthly Report of Official Travels as provided in the
same Circular; and
c) Provide the Office of the Auditor with a complete list of
serviceable municipal vehicles to ascertain and validate the
reliability of fuel consumption.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management
ensure that the utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited to
Projects, Programs, and Activities that partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Management ensure submission of
POs/contract documents not later than five (5) working days from
perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting
documents and the AIRs within 24 hours from acceptance of procured
items, in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct
the GAD Focal Person to:
a) Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report CY 2022 based on the format
prescribed in PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and submit the
same to the Auditor’s Office immediately; and
b) Henceforth, faithfully adhere to the timely submission of the GAD AR,
together with the GAD Plan and Budget, within five (5) working days
from the end of January of the preceding year.
1) Cause the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count on all the PPE owned by the Municipality
2) Instruct the same to identify the unserviceable properties and record the same in the IIRUP to facilitate the disposal and eventual dropping from the books

4) Comply with the submission of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, with complete and proper documentary requirements, within ten (10) days of the succeeding month pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 and Section 361 of GAAM;
5) Submit a Monthly Report of Official Travels as provided in the same Circular
6) Provide the Office of the Auditor with a complete list of serviceable municipal vehicles to ascertain and validate the reliability of fuel consumption.

7) We recommended that the Municipality discontinue distributing the SEF as financial assistance and instead faithfully use the fund as payment to expenses chargeable to SEF as enumerated in Section 1 of RA 5447 and DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 01,s. 2017, to ensure that the fund is for the benefit of the students and teachers alike.

8)We recommended that Management ensure the utilization of the 20% Development Fund be limited to PPAs that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
9) We recommended that Management ensure that the hiring of Contracts of Service/Job Orders, Consultants and Casual Employees be based on the necessity and urgency of the services required.
10) Further, we urge the Management to refrain from hiring of casual employees of continuance service.

Hence, we strongly reiterated our previous years’ recommendation
that Local Chief Executive:

a) Cause the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count on all the
Properties, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality;

b) Instruct the same to identify the unserviceable properties and record the
same in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
(IIRUP) to facilitate the disposal and eventual dropping from the books;
and

c) While these unserviceable properties are not yet disposed, have the
Accountant record such under Other Assets account.

Hence, we recommended that Management:

a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary
liquidation reports to concerned Source Agencies’ Accountant for
certification;

b) Reconcile Inter-Agency Payable accounts with the true status of
the involved projects;

c) And if warranted, effect the necessary adjusting entries to present
fairly the financial statements.

Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Direct the Accountant to record the purchases of supplies and
materials and other items following the Perpetual Inventory
Method pursuant to Section 51 and 114 of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Volume 1

b) Direct the Municipal Supply Officer to prepare the RIS duly
accomplished and submit for consolidation at least on a weekly
basis using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
and submit to the Accountant pursuant to Section 121 of the
NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume 1.

We recommended that Management:

a) The Municipality adhere strictly to the target collection set and
approved in the appropriation ordinance by strictly monitoring
the related receivable accounts, enforce collection thereof, and
impose penalties against delinquents, if warranted; and

b) Conduct a periodic review of collection performance to ascertain
whether targets are attained.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive cause the immediate deposit of the outstanding cash-on-hand
and strictly mandate the Treasurer to deposit intact the collections on
time.

We recommended that Management:
4. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary liquidation report accounting to the concerned National Agencies and Local Government Units; and
5. Further analyze the transactions recorded under Inter-agency Payable in relation to the projects completed and effect necessary adjusting entries to present fairly in the Financial Statements.

6. We recommended that Management require the treasury, accounting office and other concerned offices to submit the mentioned documents and strictly adhere with the prescribed time frame as required under Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 7 of COA Circular No. 94-001

7. We recommended that Management adopt measures to strengthen its system in providing financial assistance to individuals or group, in compliance to section 27 of P.D No 1445 otherwise known as the State Audit Code of the Philippines and Section 168 (c) volume 1 Government Accounting Manual (GAM).
8. We also recommended that Management direct the concerned officials and employees to submit the mentioned documents to enable us to make a decision in audit.

9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt the provisions cited above to fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure health protection and preserve environment so as not endanger the lives of the constituents.
10. Adopt the Eco Bottle Project being implemented by other LGUs. Also, explore the idea of collaborating with the nearby municipalities for the sharing construction of sanitary landfill.

11. We recommended that Management strictly comply with relevant provisions of law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for government resources and ensure that it is utilize properly, regularly and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.
12. Further, we recommended that Management direct the concerned officials and employees to submit the LDRRMFIP every beginning of the year.

13. We recommended that Management ensure that the hiring of Contracts of Service/Job Orders, Consultants and Casual Employees be based on the necessity and urgency of the services required.
14. Further, we urge the Management to refrain from hiring of casual employees of continuance service.

We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to:
15. Prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements within the prescribed period for verification; and
16. Expedite the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements of the remaining bank accounts so that reconciling items could be determined and adjustments be made in the books of the LGUs, to ensure timely feedback and monitoring mechanism.

3) We recommended that Management submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team and reconcile the same with the accounting records to establish the correctness and existence of the reported balances in the financial statements.

4) We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the accounts that are dormant, exert their effort to locate the records or take necessary actions to drop them from the books pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001.

10) We recommended that Management comply with relevant provisions of law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for government resources and ensure that it is utilize properly, regularly and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.

1.6 We reiterated our prior year recommendation that Management cause
the immediate physical count on all the Property, Plant and Equipment
owned by the Municipality;

1.7 Identify unserviceable properties and record the same in the Inventory
and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to facilitate the
disposal and eventual dropping from the book; and

1.8 While these unserviceable properties are not yet disposed, record them
under Other Assets account.

2.4 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit the necessary liquidation report accounting to the concerned
National Agencies and Local Government Units; and

2.5 Further analyze the transactions recorded under Inter-Agency Payable
in relation to the projects completed and effect necessary adjusting
entries to present fairly in the Financial Statements.

3.8 We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to:
a) Prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements
within the prescribed period for verification; and
b) Expedite the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements
of the remaining bank accounts so that reconciling items could
be determined and adjustments be made in the books of the
LGUs, to ensure timely feedback and monitoring mechanism.

4.5 We recommended that Management faithfully comply with the relevant
provisions of law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for
government resources and ensure that it is utilize properly, regularly
and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.

4.6 Further, we recommended that Management direct the concerned
officials and employees to submit the LDRRMFIP every beginning of the
year.

5.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt the provisions cited
above to fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure
health protection and preserve environment so as not endanger the lives of
the constituents;

5.7 We further recommended the Management to adopt the Eco Bottle Project
being implemented by other LGUs; and explore the idea of collaborating
with the nearby municipalities for the sharing construction of sanitary landfill.

6.6 We recommended that Management ensure that the hiring of Contracts
of Service/Job Orders, Consultants and Casual Employees be based on the
necessity and urgency of the services required;

6.7 Further, we urge the Management to refrain from hiring of casual
employees of continuance service.

7.6 We recommended that Management require the treasury, accounting
office and other concerned offices to submit the mentioned documents and
faithfully adhere with the prescribed time frame as required under Section
7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 7 of COA Circular No.
94-001.

8.5 We recommended that Management adopt measures to strengthen its
system in providing financial assistance to individuals or group, in
compliance to section 27 of P.D No 1445 otherwise known as the State
Audit Code of the Philippines and Section 168 (c) volume 1 Government
Accounting Manual (GAM).

1) We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements within the prescribed period for verification.
2) Further, the Municipal Accountant should expedite the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements, so that reconciling items could be determined and adjustments be made in the books of the LGUs, to ensure timely feedback and monitoring mechanism.

5) We recommended that the municipal government shall strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.1, s. 2005 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010 in the utilization of its 20% Economic Development Fund.

6) We recommended that Management direct the General Service Office (GSO) to maintain the needed documents in the determination of the propriety of issuance of gasoline for monitoring as well as regulation of the fuel consumption and their usage.
7) The Accounting unit should review transaction documents to ensure completeness and validity of supporting documents before payments is made. Observe the guidelines in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 prescribing the use of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Motor Vehicles.

8) We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
9) We recommended further that Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents within the period prescribed by law to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the said contracts.

11) We recommended that Management provide the Audit Team a GAD Accomplishment Report with accompanying requirements and ensure compliance with Section 5.2 of DBM-NEDA-Joint Circular No. 2004-1 to facilitate timely review of disbursements charged thereto.

12) We recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league, and for fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the League of Municipalities.
13) We recommended the Management submit the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within sixty days from receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should jointly perform review the accounts and if it is no longer possible to act on it due to the absence of the documents, request for the write-off.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.
observations.reason: Lack of coordinating coordination LGUs to with other

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to prepare
and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements within the prescribed
period for verification.

1.9 Further, the Municipal Accountant should expedite the preparation of
the Bank Reconciliation Statements, so that reconciling items could be
determined and adjustments be made in the books of the LGUs, to ensure
timely feedback and monitoring mechanism.

2.5 We recommended that Management submit the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team
and reconcile the same with the accounting records to establish the
correctness and existence of the reported balances in the financial
statements.

3.7 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
review and analyze the accounts that are dormant, exert their effort to
locate the records or take necessary actions to drop them from the books
pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001.

4.5 We recommended that the municipal government shall strictly adhere to
the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.1, s. 2005
and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-138 dated December 2, 2010
in the utilization of its 20% Economic Development Fund.

5.5 We recommended that Management direct the General Service Office
(GSO) to maintain the needed documents in the determination of the
propriety of issuance of gasoline for monitoring as well as regulation of
the fuel consumption and their usage. The Accounting unit should review
transaction documents to ensure completeness and validity of supporting
documents before payments is made. Observe the guidelines in COA
Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 prescribing the use of the
Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Motor Vehicles.

6.4 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

6.5 We recommended further that Local Chief Executive require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of perfected contracts and its
supporting documents within the period prescribed by law to facilitate
timely review and evaluation of the said contracts.

7.7 We recommended that Management comply strictly with relevant
provisions of law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for
government resources and ensure that it is utilize properly, regularly and
legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.

8.3 We recommended that Management provide the Audit Team a GAD
Accomplishment Report with accompanying requirements and ensure
compliance with Section 5.2 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No.
2004-1 to facilitate timely review of disbursements charged thereto.

9.5 We recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an
appropriation for LGU leagues contributions for its continued existence
and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its
programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a
disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an
Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the
league, and for fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be
demanded by the concerned LGU to the League of Municipalities.

10.3 We recommended the Management submit the Agency Action Plan and
Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within sixty days from receipt of the
COA Annual Audit Report.
We recommended that Local Chief Executive should strictly adhere to the general policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development Fund.
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare a Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and the Municipal Accountant to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should jointly perform to review the accounts and if it is no longer possible to act on it due to the absence of the documents, request for the write-off of these accounts from the Commission on Audit with sufficient supporting documents and justifications.
observations.managementAction: Management is finding measure to possibly request for a write-off.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt  of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund  transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.
observations.managementAction: Management is coordinating  LGUs to establish financial contributions to league of LGUs as a source of fund.
observations.reason: Lack of coordination with other LGUs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and take necessary actions to drop them from the books pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001.

We recommended that Management  require the Municipal and Development Officer to strictly adhere the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and ensure that 20% Development Fund be fully utilized to achieve the optimum desirable socio-economic and environmental development.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with relevant provisions of law and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for government resources  and ensure that it is utilize properly, regularly and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.

We recommended that Management provide the Audit Team a GAD Accomplishment Report with accompanying requirements and ensure compliance with Section 5.2 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 to facilitate timely review of disbursements charged thereto.

We recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by
LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as  proof of receipt  of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund  transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.
We recommended that Management submit the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) within sixty days from receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report.
We recommend that Local Chief Executive should strictly adhere to the general policies and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development Fund.
Direct the LDRRMO to  prepare a Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and the Municipal Accountant to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive should strictly adhere to the general policies
and guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development Fund.

Direct the LDRRMO to prepare a Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and
the Municipal Accountant to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should jointly perform
to review the accounts and if it is no longer possible to act on it due to the absence of the
documents, request for the write-off of these accounts from the Commission on Audit with
sufficient supporting documents and justifications.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for
LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continued existence and for
carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and
efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask
for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the
event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the
concerned LGU to the league.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically on the reporting for the LDRRMF. The Municipal Accountant should be directed to set up a special trust fund account for any unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to properly track the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: A special trust fund account was maintained at the Land Bank of the Philippines with account no. 1262-130-114
A negotiation is being made with the GSIS management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.
The officials concern were advised to comply within 90 days with the requirements and submit Settlement and submit suspension Settlement and submit them to the Auditor Concern
